name: SCRIMAD Group
capital_use_of_funds: Seasonal crop purchase working capital; processing capacity
crs_notes: CFC working capital financing documented. First FairTrade-certified lychee exporter in Madagascar. Strong cert stack. Subsidiary MPE (frozen purees) adds revenue diversification. Family-owned structure typical of mid-tier Madagascar exporters. No direct CRS programme link identified — approach via sector channels.
notes: One of the most certified exporters in Madagascar's spice/fruit sector. CFC precedent confirms lenders are comfortable with the entity. Ethiquable (France) is a documented buyer — useful for revenue validation. Direct approach via website. First mover advantage if CRS can establish relationship before other DFIs.

name: Madagascar Spices (Dylan Randriamihaja)
capital_use_of_funds: Vanilla purchasing season working capital; cooperative expansion
crs_notes: Family business with four-generation vanilla heritage. Small but fully certified and export-direct. Cooks Vanilla is a named buyer — useful revenue anchor. Very small scale (32 farmers, 3ha) — likely below minimum ticket unless aggregated. Named contact (Dylan) is a strong entry point.
sme_classification_note: Scale likely below minimum ticket; consider as part of aggregated SAVA vanilla facility
notes: Very small but clean profile. Direct contact available. Would benefit from aggregated lending alongside other SAVA vanilla SMEs rather than standalone facility. Strong certification baseline means TA need is primarily financial documentation.

name: MadaMarket Export
crs_notes: Bulk exporter purchasing direct from farmers/cooperatives. Claims no middlemen and in-house processing. Voatsiperifery specialist positioning is a premium niche signal. Insufficient financial data for tier assessment — company profile and scale unclear beyond website.
notes: Insufficient data for lending assessment. Approach via website to establish scale. Voatsiperifery positioning could be interesting for premium niche facility if scale is confirmed.

name: Robust Madagascar
crs_notes: 25+ year operating history is a positive longevity signal. Broad product range (spices, oils, pulses, nuts) suggests aggregator model with cross-commodity working capital needs. No certification information publicly confirmed — a gap for export-market lending. Direct email contact available.
notes: Long operating history but minimal public financial data. Approach via email to establish scale and financial profile. Certification gap is a barrier for premium-market lending.

name: EKL Export
crs_notes: Franco-Malagasy structure may imply European co-ownership — worth confirming entity structure. Works with Ecocert and HACCP-certified labs (CTHT). Full traceability claim. Broad product range (spices + cocoa + coffee + oils) means diversified working capital exposure. France is primary market — EU standards compliance confirmed implicitly.
sme_classification_note: Confirm entity structure and whether Franco-Malagasy co-ownership affects lending jurisdiction
notes: CTHT and Ecocert working relationships are strong quality signals. Agroecology sourcing approach aligns with CRS environment mandate. Financial profile entirely unknown — needs first contact to assess lending readiness.

name: Madaroma Spice
primary_products: Various spices and seasoned food products
crs_notes: Claims direct relationships with small producers and fair trade practices, though no formal certification confirmed. Mixed local/international market exposure reduces revenue predictability for working capital lending. Insufficient data for meaningful lending assessment.
notes: Placeholder entry. Needs first contact to establish product focus, scale, and financial documentation baseline.

name: TSIFA Products
crs_notes: Based in Maroantsetra — remote high-rainfall area; geographic isolation is a sourcing moat but also an operational risk for lending. Family trader model with local warehouse. Community rice programme suggests some social mission overlap with CRS. Minimal public financial data.
notes: Remote location creates both sourcing advantage and operational complexity. Community rice programme is a social signal worth exploring. Insufficient data for lending tier elevation.

name: Sahanala
revenue_source: Claude estimate (SAVAN JV scale implied by ADM partnership)
has_audited_accounts: Unknown — WFTO membership and ADM JV suggest investor-reviewed financials
capital_use_of_funds: Farmer pre-financing; working capital for vanilla and Voatsiperifery purchasing season
crs_notes: CRS is a named partner in the TSIRO Alliance — a $5.8M USAID-funded public-private partnership (2021–2026) with Sahanala. This is the highest-priority CRS relationship in Madagascar. 18,000+ farmer members is the largest farmer network of any tracked entity in the three-country pipeline. ADM (via SAVAN JV) is the primary corporate buyer — provides strong offtake security. WFTO membership and blockchain traceability underpin credibility. Sahanala likely above SME ceiling on consolidated basis — assess whether a lending facility could ring-fence a specific federation or product line.
sme_classification_note: Likely above SME ceiling on consolidated basis — consider ring-fencing a specific federation or product line (e.g. Voatsiperifery, ginger) for a sub-facility
notes: Highest-priority entry in the Madagascar pipeline. CRS has an active TSIRO programme relationship — this is the strongest warm entry point in the country. ADM offtake via SAVAN JV significantly de-risks revenue. Blockchain traceability and 600,000 hectares of protected area management are standout environment credentials. Scale may require creative structuring to fit SME facility parameters.

name: Jacarandas Spices & Ingredients
capital_use_of_funds: Smallholder pre-financing; clove and turmeric purchasing season working capital
crs_notes: 20+ years operating history with strong certification stack (EU/NOP Organic, Fair for Life, FSSC22000). 1,000+ farmer network across east coast is a meaningful livelihoods footprint. Brickaville turmeric network (est. 2016) shows ability to develop new commodity supply chains. GEGM membership (clove exporters) provides sector standing. No direct CRS link — approach via sector.
notes: One of the strongest non-vanilla candidates in the Madagascar pipeline. Clove and turmeric focus provides diversification. FSSC22000 implies food safety systems that support financial documentation culture. Approach via website — no specific contact listed. Strong alignment with multi-spice facility mandate.

name: Fanohana Cooperative (Union Fanohana)
has_audited_accounts: Unknown — Fairtrade/SPP certification implies annual audits
capital_use_of_funds: Pre-harvest farmer financing; certification maintenance; processing equipment
crs_notes: AVSF partner (AVSF = long-standing CRS-adjacent development actor in Madagascar). Ethiquable (France) is a documented fair-trade buyer — offtake risk partially mitigated. IFAD-supported spice processing investment validates institutional interest. Most certification-rich cooperative in the RCCE network. SPP certification (Small Producers Symbol) signals smallholder-specific fair trade alignment — strong CRS livelihoods story.
notes: Highest-quality cooperative in the RCCE network with the most documented certification, buyer, and donor relationships. AVSF relationship is a warm entry point via AVSF Madagascar office. Ethiquable buyer relationship provides revenue anchor. Key candidate for pilot cooperative lending in Madagascar. SPP + FLO dual certification is rare and signals premium market access.

name: Natural Spices
crs_notes: Lacon organic and FLO Cert Fairtrade since 2017 — relatively recent certifications. Direct farmer relationships claimed. Insufficient scale data for lending assessment. Approach via website to confirm volume.
notes: Certifications are real differentiators but scale is unknown. Lacon is a European certification body — may indicate EU buyer relationships. Needs scale confirmation before lending tier elevation.

name: Mananara Biosphere Cooperatives (KOMAM / COPPVM / CPVM)
has_audited_accounts: Unknown — Fairtrade certification implies annual audits
capital_use_of_funds: Pre-harvest farmer financing; collection and processing
crs_notes: Mananara Nord Biosphere Reserve context creates strong environment premium positioning. Pronatec AG (Switzerland) is the exclusive buyer — single-buyer dependency is a revenue concentration risk. Fairtrade since 2006 (KOMAM) — long track record. No independent web presence means Pronatec documentation is the primary data source. Approach via Pronatec or Fairtrade Africa.
sme_classification_note: Single-buyer (Pronatec) dependency is a concentration risk — confirm whether cooperatives have alternative offtake routes
notes: Biosphere Reserve designation is a compelling environment story for CRS. Long Fairtrade track record (KOMAM since 2006) suggests financial discipline. Pronatec relationship is the primary commercial anchor — confirm exclusivity and buyer willingness to support lending facility before proceeding.

name: Floribis
crs_notes: 25+ years operating history in Vohemar (SAVA). ISO 22000 food safety certification signals operational maturity. Works with local farmer associations. Essential oils alongside spices adds revenue diversification. Insufficient financial data publicly available.
notes: Long-established Vohemar processor — ISO 22000 is a meaningful systems signal. Approach via website. Upgrade to Tier 2 pending scale and financial documentation confirmation.

name: Vel't
crs_notes: Voatsiperifery specialist positioning is commercially compelling given growing premium demand. Organic and Fair Trade certified. Sustainable forest-management protocols for wild pepper harvesting align with CRS environment mandate. Scale entirely unknown — needs first contact.
notes: Wild pepper specialist with environment credentials — interesting for a premium niche facility. Certifications in place but scale unknown. Voatsiperifery forest harvesting protocols are a natural CRS environment story.

name: Epices des Iles
crs_notes: 35+ year operating history is a notable longevity signal. Ecocert organic certification. Limited public digital presence — website status uncertain. One of the older pre-Fairtrade wave exporters. Scale and current financial status unknown.
notes: Long history but low web presence — confirm whether still actively trading before investment in relationship.

name: Trimeta Agro Food
crs_notes: Part of Trimeta Group conglomerate — subsidiary status raises question of consolidated group financials vs. standalone entity lending. FSSC22000 implies institutional food safety systems. Likely at the larger end of the SME spectrum. Conglomerate ownership may complicate lending structure.
sme_classification_note: Conglomerate subsidiary — confirm whether Trimeta Group consolidated revenue exceeds SME ceiling
notes: Conglomerate structure complicates SME classification. FSSC22000 is a positive systems signal. Deprioritise until standalone entity and financials can be confirmed.

name: Promabio
crs_notes: SYMABIO member (Madagascar organic syndicate) — sector standing confirmed. Organic certified. Very limited public profile beyond these signals. Website status uncertain.
notes: SYMABIO membership is useful for sector outreach. Very low profile — approach via SYMABIO network contact.

name: Lopat Vanilla
crs_notes: Traditional Antalaha-based trader — likely multi-generational farmer relationships. No certification confirmed. Antalaha hub means proximity to other SAVA SMEs. Minimal public profile.
notes: Placeholder entry. Approach via Antalaha sector contacts or RCCE if any cooperative affiliation confirmed.

name: Pure Vanilla
crs_notes: Nearly 50-year Sambava vanilla family — one of the longest track records in SAVA. Nielsen-Massey supplier relationship is a strong commercial anchor (NM is a leading global vanilla brand). US and EU market access confirmed. FLO-Cert Fairtrade and Lacon organic give premium market access. Nielsen-Massey relationship provides indirect revenue validation.
notes: Long track record + Nielsen-Massey buyer relationship = one of the stronger vanilla-focused SME lending candidates. Approach via website. Financial documentation is the primary unknown. Consider alongside other Sambava-based exporters for aggregated SAVA facility.

name: Ile d'Or Company
crs_notes: Ecocert organic and Fairtrade certified. Multi-market export reach (Europe, USA, Asia, Africa) suggests meaningful scale — though specific volume unknown. Fairtrade premium distribution directly to growers is a livelihoods signal. Approach via website.
notes: Broad export geography is a positive scale signal. Needs first contact to confirm volume and financial documentation.

name: HEP (Huile Essentielle des Pangalanes)
crs_notes: Atsinanana region presence (same region as MITSINJO cooperative) suggests possible supply chain overlap or complementarity. Multi-spice range aligns with facility mandate. Specific certification bodies not publicly confirmed. 10-year operating history modest but real.
notes: Atsinanana positioning is interesting given MITSINJO cooperative presence in same region. Approach via website to confirm certifications and export volumes.

name: Lazaro
crs_notes: Linked to the '1000 Planters of Madagascar' cooperative (est. 2024) — very new cooperative structure. Broad export market geography suggests meaningful scale, but no financial data publicly available. New cooperative relationship is worth monitoring for future CRS engagement.
notes: New cooperative link (2024) is worth tracking. Broad export geography suggests a functional export business — needs first contact for financial profile.

name: Vanilla Island Company
crs_notes: Sambava-based processor with in-house factory and annual export programme. No certification information confirmed. Low public profile beyond company website.
notes: In-house processing factory is a positive infrastructure signal. Approach via website to confirm scale and certifications.

name: Vanilla Special Export
crs_notes: Very new entrant (2022). Three years of operating history is insufficient for lending assessment without supporting financial data. Deprioritise until track record established.
sme_classification_note: Founded 2022 — insufficient operating history for lending assessment
notes: Too new for lending consideration without documented track record. Monitor and revisit 2027.

name: Vanileco
crs_notes: European-Malagasy partnership structure — confirm entity jurisdiction and whether European co-ownership affects lending eligibility. 10-year operating history. Claims sustainable practices and social responsibility. Global partner network stated. No certification detail confirmed.
sme_classification_note: Confirm entity jurisdiction — European co-ownership may affect lending eligibility
notes: Entity structure confirmation needed before lending assessment. Approach via website.

name: Riavanille Sarl
crs_notes: Founded 2021 — limited operating history. Cloves alongside vanilla adds modest product diversification. Antalaha-based — SAVA hub proximity. Insufficient data for lending assessment.
notes: New entrant. Insufficient operating history and financial data for lending consideration. Monitor.

name: Videeko Vanilla
crs_notes: Most certified vanilla exporter in the Madagascar dataset — Organic, Kosher, HACCP, Fairtrade, ISO 22000, SA8000, Sedex. SA8000 (labour standards) is extremely rare in the Madagascar spice sector and suggests sophisticated institutional buyer relationships. Boston, USA contact presence implies active North American market engagement. Scale unknown but certification depth suggests meaningful revenue.
notes: SA8000 and Sedex membership strongly suggest institutional/multinational buyer relationships — likely significant export volume. Scale confirmation is the primary missing piece. Approach via website or Boston contact. High potential if scale confirmed.

name: La Saveur Vanille
crs_notes: 30+ year operating history in Antalaha — longevity signal in a volatile sector. Emphasises sustainability, traceability, and community investment. No specific certifications publicly confirmed — a gap. Global partner network stated. Approach via website.
notes: Long history but no certified buyer relationships confirmed. Certification and financial documentation are the primary gaps. Approach via website.

name: Madagascar Vanilla (SAVA SARL)
crs_notes: Subsidiary of SAVA SARL — confirm parent entity and consolidated revenue before lending assessment. Growers' cooperative sourcing relationship is positive. Direct-from-producer Bourbon vanilla positioning. Very limited financial data.
sme_classification_note: Subsidiary — confirm SAVA SARL parent entity structure and consolidated financials
notes: Parent entity structure needs clarification. Cooperative sourcing is a positive livelihoods signal. Deprioritise until entity and financial profile clarified.

name: AgriExport Madagascar
crs_notes: Frames itself as a technical and digital support ecosystem for Malagasy agri-food producers. Comprehensive spice range (five pepper types). Antananarivo base suggests aggregator-exporter model rather than direct farmer sourcing. Insufficient data for lending assessment.
notes: Comprehensive spice range is interesting for multi-spice facility. Digital/tech positioning may indicate more sophisticated financial systems than peers. Approach via website.

name: Flavors & Commodities
crs_notes: Voatsiperifery and organic vanilla specialist. Organic positioning confirmed for primary products. Very limited public profile beyond product offer. One of several Voatsiperifery specialists in the pipeline.
notes: Voatsiperifery specialist — premium niche signal. Approach via website to confirm scale.

name: Malagasy Company Product (MCP)
crs_notes: Antananarivo-based aggregator-exporter offering Voatsiperifery, vanilla, cloves, honey, and mixed spices for B2B wholesale. Natural/compliant product positioning stated. Very limited profile.
notes: Minimal profile. Approach via website only after higher-priority entries are exhausted.

name: APL Vanilla & Spices (APLV)
crs_notes: Family-owned grower-to-exporter with SAVA sourcing and Antananarivo HQ. Vanille BV partner profile suggests European buyer relationships. Active trade show presence implies commercial ambition. No certification detail confirmed — a gap.
notes: Vertically integrated claim and Vanille BV listing are modest positive signals. Certification and financial documentation are the main gaps. Approach via website or trade show contacts.

name: Dago Vanilla
crs_notes: North America focus (Canada + USA) with Organic and HACCP certifications. Claims to be among first Madagascar vanilla exporters with HACCP certification. GAP programme compliance and cooperative farmer sourcing are positive livelihoods signals. Scale entirely unknown.
notes: HACCP and organic certifications with North American market focus are solid signals. GAP programme and rural cooperative sourcing align with CRS livelihoods mandate. Needs scale confirmation.

name: MITSINJO Cooperative
has_audited_accounts: Unknown — Fairtrade/SPP certification implies annual audits
capital_use_of_funds: Pre-harvest farmer financing; turmeric and ginger purchasing; certification maintenance
crs_notes: AVSF partner — same warm entry point as Fanohana (mdg-010). RCCE network member. Pronatec buyer relationship provides offtake security. Turmeric + ginger + cinnamon focus means non-vanilla commodity exposure — useful for portfolio diversification within a Madagascar facility. SPP + FLO dual certification. Brickaville district location (Atsinanana) is different from SAVA vanilla cluster — geographic reach is an asset.
notes: Second-highest-priority cooperative entry after Fanohana. AVSF warm entry point, Pronatec buyer anchor, and SPP/FLO certification stack are all strong. Non-vanilla commodity focus (turmeric, ginger) is a differentiator for portfolio diversification. Brickaville district is geographically distinct — consider alongside Fanohana for a two-cooperative pilot.

name: Lafaza
existing_debt: CRS impact investment (Miller Center, 2017 — undisclosed amount)
has_audited_accounts: Unknown — Fairtrade USA certification implies annual audits; CRS/Miller Center investment implies investor-reviewed financials
capital_use_of_funds: Vanilla and Voatsiperifery purchasing season working capital; farmer pre-financing
crs_notes: CRS has a documented direct lending/investment relationship via the Miller Center impact investment partnership (2017). This is CRS's only confirmed Madagascar entity with prior direct financial engagement. Hand in Hand International is a co-partner for farmer training. Fairtrade USA certified since 2014 — among the earliest in Madagascar. 500+ farmer network with above-market price payments and Fairtrade premium redistribution. Strongest livelihoods story of any private company (non-cooperative) in the pipeline. US market access confirmed (direct). Voatsiperifery alongside vanilla gives product diversification.

donor_history.donor: CRS / Miller Center for Social Entrepreneurship

notes: Highest-priority private company (non-cooperative) entry in the Madagascar pipeline. CRS has a prior direct financial relationship — the strongest warm entry point alongside Sahanala. Fairtrade USA certification since 2014 implies consistent financial reporting culture. Above-market farmer prices and premium redistribution are standout livelihoods credentials. Voatsiperifery + vanilla product mix de-risks vanilla price volatility. First outreach call.

name: MC Ingredients (MCI)
primary_contact: Mr. Hervol (co-founder, MBA Global Management); Mrs. Rajaona (co-founder, MSc international business & agriculture)
capital_use_of_funds: Vanilla and clove purchasing season working capital; chili production scale-up
crs_notes: Founded by a US Peace Corps Madagascar alumni (2006–07) and a Malagasy agribusiness partner — US-Malagasy co-ownership structure is a differentiator for anglophone market access. Certified organic exporter. Strategically located in Toamasina (Madagascar's main port) — ideal for clove container export. African Bird's Eye Chili produced organically on own project site near Moramanga. Active CSR (school rehabilitation, DREDD reforestation partnership). Appeared on private partners list from CRS-connected colleague — worth direct outreach.
notes: Co-founder's Peace Corps background and 15+ years Madagascar experience give strong local network. Toamasina port location is a logistics advantage for cloves. Chili production adds non-vanilla income stream. Approach via mcingredients.com. Confirmed as 'MCI' on colleague's private partners list.

name: Phael Flor Export
capital_use_of_funds: Seasonal crop purchasing; plantation expansion (100ha pepper, 200ha cinnamon); essential oil distillation capacity
crs_notes: 45+ year operating history — one of the longest-established multi-spice processors in Madagascar. Own plantations: 100ha pepper (Nosy Varika), 200ha cinnamon (Vatomandry/Mahanoro), 10-acre ginger (Moramanga), vanilla sourcing (Antalaha/Sambava/Mananara). Two Antananarivo processing factories. Ecocert organic and fair trade certified. EU and US market access confirmed. CSR: 4 classrooms built, road maintenance in production areas. Strong alignment with CRS livelihoods + environment mandate.
notes: One of the most substantively documented non-cooperative spice processors in the pipeline. Own plantations (not just aggregation) means stronger collateral story for lending. Ecocert certification and 45-year track record are strong credit signals. Essential oils alongside spices provide revenue diversification. Approach via website — CEO contact is Rolland Ramboatiana.

name: Akesson's Organic
crs_notes: 2,000-hectare family estate in Sambirano Valley — estate model, not smallholder aggregator. Supplies 300+ chocolate makers globally including Dandelion and Rózsavölgyi. Primarily a sector anchor and premium buyer reference point, not a lending target. SME classification unlikely — estate scale and vertically self-contained model make debt facility structuring complex. Note as sector reference only.
sme_classification_note: Large family estate — not an SME lending target. Retain as sector anchor reference only.
notes: Sector anchor. Estate-based model with own supply chain. Not a borrower prospect but useful as market reference and introduction point to premium international buyers. Swedish-Malagasy family, estate roots since 1920. Solar energy conversion completed. Appeared on colleague's private partners list.

name: Mada Spices
crs_notes: CONFIRMED DIFFERENT from Madaroma Spice (mdg-006). A trade directory lists a 'Mada Spices' at Lot IBM 95 Tsaralalana, Antananarivo (contact: Dev Dodhia) with an eclectic mix of metals, chemicals and spices — suggesting a general trader rather than a spice specialist. The logo on the colleague's partner list may refer to a different, more specialised entity with the same or similar name. Ask colleague to identify directly.
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please confirm the identity of 'Mada Spices' from the partner list image. Confirmed distinct from Madaroma Spice. A general trading entity with this name exists in Antananarivo but may not be the same company shown on the logo. No website or spice-specific profile found.

name: Toly Hare
org_type: Unknown (possibly farmer group or small cooperative)
crs_notes: Listed in pseau.org (water/sanitation sector database) as based in Toliara — suggests community development or WASH programme involvement alongside any agricultural activity. Confirmed southwest location by colleague. Southwest Madagascar (Atsimo-Andrefana) is not a primary spice-producing region — may deal in endemic dryland crops or spice-adjacent products. Very small per colleague's assessment.
sme_classification_note: Very small — below minimum ticket without aggregation
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please confirm what Toly Hare does, their products, and how they appeared on the partner list. Southwest location is unusual for spice sector. Pseau.org listing suggests community/WASH angle. Colleague described as 'very small'.

name: HCP
crs_notes: Logo identified on colleague's private partners list. No public web presence or sector information found. Initials only — full company name unknown.
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please identify HCP — full name, location, products, and role in the spice sector. No public information found.

name: Diary Farm
crs_notes: Logo identified on colleague's private partners list. Name suggests possible dairy operation rather than spice sector — may be a diversified farm with spice component, or the name may be phonetic/transliterated from Malagasy. No public web presence found.
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please clarify what Diary Farm does and whether it has a spice sector role. Name may suggest dairy — confirm products and relevance to spice lending facility.

name: Bionex
crs_notes: Logo identified on colleague's private partners list. Name suggests biological/natural extracts company — possibly essential oils, phytochemicals, or bio-ingredients. No public web presence found.
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please identify Bionex — full name, location, and products. Name suggests bio-extract or essential oil company.

name: Fairoils
crs_notes: Logo identified on colleague's private partners list. Name strongly suggests a fair trade essential oils producer/exporter — spice-adjacent and potentially in scope for a multi-commodity facility. No public web presence found.
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please identify Fairoils — location, products, and certifications. Name suggests fair trade oils — potentially in scope if essential oils are included in facility mandate.

name: Beelson Group
crs_notes: Logo identified on colleague's private partners list. 'Group' designation suggests a multi-entity business. No public web presence or sector profile found.
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please identify Beelson Group — full name, location, products, and sector. No public information found.

name: Flora Mad
primary_products: Unknown (possibly botanical extracts or aromatic plants)
crs_notes: Logo identified on colleague's private partners list. Name suggests botanical/aromatic plant focus — possibly essential oils or dried botanical ingredients. 'Mad' abbreviation for Madagascar is commonly used. No public web presence found.
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please identify Flora Mad — full name, location, and products.

name: Groupe Basan
crs_notes: Logo identified on colleague's private partners list as 'Basan' or 'Groupe Basan'. Group structure suggests a multi-entity conglomerate. No public web presence or sector profile found.
sme_classification_note: Group structure — confirm if any spice subsidiary would be the lending counterparty
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please identify Groupe Basan — sector, products, and whether they have a spice-specific arm. Group structures complicate SME classification.

name: Gehem
crs_notes: Logo identified on colleague's private partners list — circular badge design. No public web presence or sector profile found. Name may be an acronym.
notes: ⚠ ASK COLLEAGUE: Please identify Gehem — full name (possibly an acronym), location, and products.
